articles.title: 📋 São Paulo named for new Copa Sul-Americana test
articles.description: It’s a Copa Sudamericana night for São Paulo. Tricolor visit Chilean side O'Higgins at Estadio El Teniente in Rancagua. Kickoff is set for 7 p.m. (Brasília time). And it is valid for the fourth round of Group C in the competition. Yael Falcón (ARG) is the referee. ESPN and Disney+ will broadcast the match. 🇨🇱 O'Higgins São Paulo’s rival is enjoying a good spell. They won their last Sudamericana match, their last two fixtures in the Chilean league, and lead their group in the Copa Chile after drawing in the last round. This Thursday’s clash is crucial, as they have six points and will then play two away matches. Millonarios (four points) and Boston River (zero) will also play at the same time. Lucas Bovaglio rested some starters in the cup match precisely to have his strongest lineup available. 🇾🇪 São Paulo Tricolor lead the group with seven points and could move very close to securing first place - which guarantees direct qualification to the round of 16. Roger Machado has a total of seven players out injured - including Lucas Moura, who is out for the season. Marcos Antônio, Lucas Ramon, Pablo Maia, Alan Franco, Tolói, and Maik are the other names in the medical department. Only Bobadilla was rested and did not even travel. But the lineup does in fact feature several backups and academy products from Cotia. The idea is to use the main available players if needed. Photo: Reprodução/São Paulo This article was translated into English by Artificial Intelligence. You can read the original version in 🇧🇷 here.
